summary: Reconciled durable docs and operational state with the current homepage: the banner is documented as a CSS-only gradient treatment, the Schedule section is now Program, and the homepage agenda is recorded as generic provisional content rather than placeholder schedule copy.
work_completed: Reviewed the referenced Builder result packets alongside the live `index.html` and `assets/main.css` state before editing docs.
Updated `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md` to describe the CSS-only banner, Program navigation/section naming, current logistics copy, organizer contacts, and the generic full-day agenda.
Updated `docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md` to remove the `assets/atlas-banner.png` dependency description and document the restored CSS-only `.banner-panel` implementation.
Updated `.control-tower/docs/state/current-status.md` so operational status matches the Program section, generic agenda state, and banner-image rollback.

Updated `.control-tower/docs/state/decisions.md` to supersede stale inferred memory about the removed banner asset and Schedule naming, and to record the current Program/banner state for future sessions.

findings.message: Durable docs no longer describe `assets/atlas-banner.png`; both product and architecture docs now match the restored CSS-only banner implementation in `assets/main.css`.
Operational memory was also corrected so future Tower sessions do not rely on stale banner-image or Schedule-era state.
The homepage still uses a generic Program agenda and placeholder submissions copy; another docs pass will be needed once final timing, speakers, and submission instructions are confirmed.

follow_up_recommendations: Run a follow-up Scribe pass when final program timing, speakers, or submission requirements are published so the spec and status docs can replace provisional wording.
memory_worthy: Homepage hero is again a CSS-only gradient banner panel in `assets/main.css` and no longer depends on a repository banner image asset.
Homepage IA now uses `Program` instead of `Schedule`, and the Program section contains a generic full-day agenda while exact timing and speakers remain unconfirmed.
